text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID:           SP-XXXX-XXXX
Patient Name:          Okonkwo, Margaret
Date of Procedure:     [DATE]
Date Received:         [DATE]
Date Reported:         [DATE]
Submitting Physician:  Urology / Surgical Oncology
Clinical Service:      Urology
Procedure:             Right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection (open approach)
Clinical Indication:   Known clear cell renal cell carcinoma, right kidney, 11.4 cm, with perinephric fat involvement; metastatic disease (bilateral pulmonary nodules); cytoreductive intent
Accession #:           [ACCESSION]
Pathologist:           [PATHOLOGIST NAME, MD]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The patient is a 63-year-old female with a recently diagnosed right renal mass. Pre-operative staging CT demonstrated an 11.4 x 9.7 x 10.2 cm heterogeneous right renal mass involving the upper and mid poles with extension into perinephric fat, hilar and paraaortic retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and bilateral pulmonary nodules. Pre-operative percutaneous core needle biopsy confirmed clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation. She was taken to the operating room for open right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection in the setting of cytoreductive intent for stage IV ccRCC.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) RECEIVED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  A. Right kidney with perirenal fat (radical nephrectomy specimen), fresh
  B. Retroperitoneal lymph nodes, multiple nodal packets (labeled by surgeon: hilar, paraaortic superior, paraaortic inferior)

Note: Right adrenal gland was NOT submitted; preserved intraoperatively per operative report.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Part A — Right Kidney with Perirenal Fat:

Received fresh, then fixed in formalin, is a right radical nephrectomy specimen consisting of a kidney with attached perirenal adipose tissue and a segment of proximal ureter. The specimen in aggregate measures 14.5 x 11.8 x 9.0 cm and weighs 710 grams (unfixed). The external surface of the perirenal fat is inked black. The ureter is identified, measures 2.8 cm in length and 0.5 cm in diameter, and is submitted entirely as a shave margin (Section A1).

The kidney is bivalved along its longitudinal axis. The renal capsule is intact over the non-neoplastic kidney but is effaced and locally disrupted by the tumor mass. A dominant, poorly circumscribed neoplasm is identified occupying the upper and mid poles of the kidney, measuring 11.4 cm in greatest dimension (11.4 x 9.2 x 8.8 cm in three orthogonal dimensions). The cut surface of the tumor is heterogeneous, with a bright yellow-orange to tan periphery of viable-appearing solid tumor interspersed with irregular zones of central chalky-white and hemorrhagic necrosis accounting for approximately 30% of the total tumor area. Small foci of firmer, white-gray tissue are noted at the periphery of the neoplasm, morphologically consistent with areas of sarcomatoid transformation identified in the prior biopsy.

The tumor extends grossly into the perirenal adipose tissue. Surgical margins are assessed as follows:
- Perinephric fat (radial soft tissue margin): focally involved by tumor at one pole (inked black; minimal clearance, less than 1 mm at the closest point, though no frank transection of tumor is identified)
- Gerota's fascia: submitted separately; appears grossly intact
- Renal sinus fat: the tumor extends into and involves the renal sinus fat at the hilum
- Renal vein margin: the renal vein stump is present, measures 1.4 cm in circumference at the staple line; serial sectioning reveals no gross tumor within the renal vein lumen; margin submitted as a shave section (Section A2)
- Ureteral margin: see A1 above

The non-neoplastic renal parenchyma is grossly unremarkable. The cortex is tan-pink. No additional discrete masses identified.

Representative sections submitted:
  A1 — Ureteral shave margin
  A2 — Renal vein shave margin
  A3-A4 — Tumor with closest perirenal fat margin (inked)
  A5 — Tumor with extension into perinephric fat
  A6 — Tumor center, areas of necrosis
  A7 — Sarcomatoid-appearing gross region at tumor periphery
  A8 — Tumor with renal sinus fat involvement
  A9 — Tumor-renal parenchyma interface
  A10 — Non-neoplastic kidney
  A11 — Renal capsule over tumor
  A12 — Additional viable tumor, yellow-tan region

Part B — Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes:

Received in formalin in three labeled containers:
  B1 (Hilar): Multiple fragments of fibroadipose tissue in aggregate measuring 5.4 x 3.8 x 1.7 cm, with 3 grossly identifiable discrete lymph nodes, the largest measuring 2.8 cm in greatest dimension. The largest node has a pale tan-gray cut surface with focal areas of necrosis consistent with metastatic involvement. Entirely submitted (3 cassettes).
  B2 (Paraaortic superior): Fibroadipose tissue measuring 4.2 x 2.9 x 1.5 cm with 2 grossly identifiable lymph nodes, the larger measuring 1.9 cm. Entire tissue submitted (2 cassettes).
  B3 (Paraaortic inferior): Fibroadipose tissue measuring 3.6 x 2.1 x 1.2 cm with 3 grossly identifiable lymph nodes, all under 1.0 cm in greatest dimension. Entire tissue submitted (2 cassettes).

Total nodes submitted from Part B: at least 8 nodes identified grossly; complete histologic assessment below.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Part A — Right Kidney:

Tumor: The primary renal neoplasm is composed predominantly of cells with abundant optically clear cytoplasm, centrally placed nuclei with prominent nucleoli, arranged in nests, alveoli, and trabeculae supported by a delicate arborizing sinusoidal vascular network, classic for clear cell renal cell carcinoma. The nuclear grade is assessed as ISUP/WHO Grade 3, defined by the presence of prominent nucleoli visible at 10x magnification in the predominant tumor component, with occasional cells with more extreme nuclear enlargement approaching grade 4 features at the sarcomatoid interface.

Sarcomatoid differentiation is identified and constitutes approximately 20% of the viable neoplasm. The sarcomatoid component is characterized by high-grade spindle cell morphology with elongated, pleomorphic nuclei, coarse chromatin, conspicuous nucleoli, and abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm arranged in fascicles and storiform patterns. Brisk mitotic activity is present in the sarcomatoid component (up to 7 mitoses per 10 high-power fields, including atypical forms). Rhabdoid differentiation is not identified.

Necrosis: Geographic and coagulative tumor necrosis is present, estimated at 30% of the sampled tumor area.

Extension into perirenal fat: Present. Irregular tongues of tumor with desmoplastic stroma extend through the renal capsule into the perinephric adipose tissue.

Renal sinus fat involvement: Present. Tumor extends along vascular channels and fibrous septa into the renal sinus adipose tissue at the hilum.

Gerota's fascia: Sections of Gerota's fascia are submitted; no definitive tumor involves the deep surface of Gerota's fascia. The fascia appears intact on available sections.

Renal vein (Part A2): Serial sections of the renal vein shave margin demonstrate no intravascular tumor thrombus. The vein wall is unremarkable. Renal vein margin: NEGATIVE.

Ureteral margin (Part A1): Sections show normal urothelium lining with no atypia and no carcinoma. Ureteral margin: NEGATIVE.

Perinephric fat margin: The tumor approaches the inked perirenal fat margin closely (less than 1 mm at the closest point) but no frank transection of tumor at the inked margin is identified. This represents an R0 resection with a focally close margin.

Non-neoplastic kidney (Part A10): The adjacent renal parenchyma shows mild cortical simplification, globally sclerosed glomeruli (approximately 15%), and mild interstitial fibrosis consistent with background nephrosclerosis in the setting of hypertension. No additional neoplasms identified.

Part B — Lymph Nodes:

Of the retroperitoneal lymph nodes submitted, a total of 8 lymph nodes are identified histologically after complete processing and sectioning:

  B1 (Hilar): 3 of 3 nodes positive for metastatic renal cell carcinoma. The largest positive node measures 2.8 cm in greatest dimension and demonstrates extranodal extension: metastatic tumor penetrates through the lymph node capsule into the perinodal adipose tissue. The metastatic deposits consist of clear cell RCC, consistent with the primary tumor. The sarcomatoid component is also identified within the metastatic deposits in the largest node.

  B2 (Paraaortic superior): 2 of 2 nodes positive for metastatic renal cell carcinoma. Metastatic clear cell RCC with focal necrosis. No extranodal extension.

  B3 (Paraaortic inferior): 0 of 3 nodes positive (3 nodes with reactive follicular hyperplasia; no carcinoma identified).

Summary: 5 of 8 retroperitoneal lymph nodes positive for metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (5/8). Extranodal extension present (largest hilar node).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES — IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Immunohistochemistry was performed on representative sections of the primary renal tumor (surgical specimen) and correlated with the prior biopsy immunoprofile. Results confirmed on CONFIRM platform using standard positive and negative controls.

  CA9 (Carbonic Anhydrase IX, clone EP161):   Diffuse strong membranous positivity (3+, >95% of clear cell component). Reduced/absent in sarcomatoid foci.
  PAX8 (clone MRQ-50):                         Diffuse nuclear positivity in both clear cell and sarcomatoid components.
  Vimentin (clone V9):                          Diffuse strong cytoplasmic positivity.
  CD10 (clone 56C6):                            Diffuse membranous/cytoplasmic positivity.
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30):                     Negative in clear cell component; rare positive cells in sarcomatoid areas.
  CK AE1/AE3 (broad-spectrum):                 Focal weak positivity in sarcomatoid component (~10% cells); diffuse in clear cell component.
  EMA (clone E29):                              Focal positive.
  TFE3 (clone MRQ-37):                          Negative (no translocation-associated overexpression).
  CAIX:                                         See CA9 above (same clone used).
  SOX10:                                        Negative (excludes sarcomatoid dedifferentiation with melanocytic/nerve sheath phenotype).
  Desmin:                                       Negative.
  SMA (alpha-smooth muscle actin):              Focal positive in spindled areas (consistent with myofibroblastic stroma, not diagnostic of leiomyosarcomatoid differentiation).

PD-L1 Immunohistochemistry:
  Assay:           Dako PD-L1 IHC 22C3 pharmDx (FDA-approved companion diagnostic assay)
  Scoring:         Combined Positive Score (CPS) methodology
  Result:          CPS = 18
  Interpretation:  PD-L1 positive (CPS ≥1). Tumor cell PD-L1 expression predominantly in the clear cell component (TPS approximately 25-30%); immune cells strongly positive contributing to the overall CPS score. PD-L1 expression notably enriched at the interface of sarcomatoid and clear cell components.

Note: Ki-67 proliferative index (clone MIB-1): approximately 35% in the high-grade/sarcomatoid component; approximately 12% in the conventional clear cell areas.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
A. RIGHT KIDNEY, RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY:
   - RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, CLEAR CELL TYPE
     ISUP/WHO NUCLEAR GRADE: 3
     SARCOMATOID DIFFERENTIATION: PRESENT (~20% of viable tumor)
     TUMOR SIZE: 11.4 cm greatest dimension
     TUMOR NECROSIS: PRESENT (approximately 30% of tumor area)
     LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: PRESENT (identified in peritumoral vascular channels)
     PERINEPHRIC FAT INVASION: PRESENT
     RENAL SINUS FAT INVASION: PRESENT
     GEROTA'S FASCIA: NOT INVOLVED (intact on available sections)
     RENAL VEIN MARGIN: NEGATIVE FOR TUMOR
     URETERAL MARGIN: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA
     PERIRENAL SOFT TISSUE MARGIN: FOCALLY CLOSE (<1 mm) BUT NEGATIVE (R0)
     ADRENAL GLAND: NOT SUBMITTED (retained intraoperatively)

B. RETROPERITONEAL LYMPH NODES:
   - METASTATIC CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA
     5 OF 8 LYMPH NODES POSITIVE (5/8)
     LARGEST POSITIVE NODE: 2.8 cm WITH EXTRANODAL EXTENSION
     LOCATION: HILAR (3/3) AND PARAAORTIC SUPERIOR (2/2)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, Kidney)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Primary Tumor (pT):    pT3a — Tumor extends into perinephric fat and/or renal sinus fat but does not extend beyond Gerota's fascia and does not extend into the ipsilateral adrenal gland
  Regional Lymph Nodes:  pN1 — Metastasis in regional lymph node(s)
  Distant Metastasis:    pM1 — Distant metastasis present (bilateral pulmonary metastases confirmed on clinical imaging; histologic confirmation of distant mets not performed at this time)
  Overall Stage:         Stage IV (pT3a pN1 pM1)

  Histologic Type:       Renal cell carcinoma, clear cell type (8310/3)
  Histologic Grade:      ISUP/WHO Grade 3
  Surgical Margins:      R0 (negative, focally close at perirenal fat margin)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The morphologic and immunophenotypic features are diagnostic of clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation. The immunoprofile (CA9+, PAX8+, CD10+, vimentin+, CK7-, TFE3-) is consistent with and confirms the clear cell RCC subtype. The sarcomatoid transformation (approximately 20% of viable tumor) is defined by the presence of malignant spindle cells with high-grade nuclear features as described above, and is a recognized adverse prognostic feature associated with aggressive clinical behavior, chemoresistance, and inferior outcomes with standard targeted therapies. The high PD-L1 CPS (18) is consistent with the prior biopsy result (CPS 15) and with the known association between sarcomatoid differentiation and upregulated PD-L1 expression in ccRCC.

Lymphrovascular invasion is identified, and lymph node metastases are extensive (5/8 nodes), including extranodal extension from the largest hilar node. These findings, in conjunction with the established distant metastatic disease, are consistent with a high metastatic burden.

The non-neoplastic kidney shows background nephrosclerotic changes consistent with long-standing hypertension.

This report has been reviewed by a second pathologist. Results are consistent with the pre-operative core biopsy diagnosis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Electronic signature: [ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST, MD, Anatomic Pathology]
Reviewed and Electronically Signed: [DATE]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
